findings.summary: utils/pipeline_template.py mixes live production code with unreachable example template functions
findings.evidence: standard_module_function (lines ~194-272) and process_single_file (lines ~273-286) import from '.shared_functions' which does not exist and reference functions not defined in scope. create_standard_module_function (lines ~288-374) similarly imports from '.shared_functions'. These are example/template code that was never wired to any real caller. grep across all 25 step scripts shows zero callers. The file also contains a second module-level docstring at line 178 starting a new 'Standardized Pipeline Module Function Template' section — two separate modules concatenated into one file.
findings.suggestion: Delete standard_module_function, process_single_file, and create_standard_module_function (approximately lines 194-374). The file already has create_standardized_pipeline_script which is the real production function.

findings.summary: execute_pipeline_step wraps a hardcoded return value in a try/except that can never trigger
findings.evidence: Lines 113-130: try block contains only StepExecutionResult(step_name=step_name, success=True, duration=0.1, output=...) which is a dataclass instantiation — it cannot raise. The except block is unreachable defensive code. The stub comment says 'In practice, this would import and execute the actual step'.
findings.suggestion: Remove the try/except wrapper. If this is a stub, document it as such and return directly. If it will eventually call real logic, add the try/except when that logic is added.

findings.summary: get_output_dir_for_script in utils/pipeline.py has a 8-line docstring for a 7-line wrapper function
findings.evidence: Lines 15-27: docstring includes 'This is a wrapper that delegates to pipeline.config.get_output_dir_for_script but avoids circular import issues', plus full Args/Returns section for a function whose body is a try/except import with one-line fallback. The docstring body-to-code ratio is >1.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: 'Delegate to pipeline.config.get_output_dir_for_script; uses fallback path on ImportError.'

findings.summary: standardized_error_handling.py error_context has a '# Create structured error' comment above pipeline_error_handler.create_error()
findings.evidence: Line 51: '# Create structured error' directly above self.pipeline_error_handler.create_error(...). The comment names the next line without adding information.

findings.summary: render/processor.py inserts paths into sys.path during processing rather than at module import time
findings.evidence: sys.path mutation during function calls creates non-deterministic import state that depends on call order. Multiple callers may see different module resolution depending on which render functions have been called previously.
findings.suggestion: Move sys.path insertions to module level or, better, use importlib with explicit path arguments. At minimum, check whether the path is already present before inserting.
